content: Tobacco use remains the leading preventable cause of death globally, causing over 8 million deaths annually including 1.3 million from secondhand smoke exposure. Tobacco-related diseases include lung cancer, COPD, cardiovascular disease, stroke, and multiple other cancers. Nicotine addiction develops through dopaminergic reward pathways, with withdrawal symptoms including irritability, anxiety, difficulty concentrating, and cravings. E-cigarettes and vaping products present emerging challenges—potentially less harmful than combustible tobacco for adult smokers but creating new addiction pathways for youth. The WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (FCTC) provides the international treaty framework. Tobacco industry tactics include targeting marginalized communities, youth-oriented marketing, and political interference.
